message.content:
In /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py (a portable copy of an acceptance harness; do NOT touch the production acceptance_outline_agent.py), FIX the assertion-vacuous THIN positive control.
Currently run_thin() (around lines 149-165) only REPORTS metrics (search_more_evidence_calls, checklist_gap_events) and never asserts anything, so the harness exits 0 even if THIN fired 0 searches — the control cannot fail.
Add an enforceable pass gate for THIN: assert that search_calls >= 1 AND the outline was mutated (outline_mutated / the outline changed vs seed). Compute an explicit boolean like valid_positive_control (mirror how the SATURATED negative control computes valid_negative_control at ~line 260). Make main() exit NON-ZERO if either control (positive THIN or negative SATURATED) fails.
Keep it minimal and self-contained; do not change pipeline runtime behavior — this is harness/control logic only. Confirm by reading back the changed region.
Return the exact new assertion code and the line range.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""W1 acceptance harness for the agentic outliner loop (I-outline-agent).
2	
3	Runs TWO real, live scenarios against src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:
4	
5	  (a) THIN  — seed evidence covers ONLY part of the research question (efficacy), the question
6	      itself also asks about long-term cardiovascular safety. Acceptance: the checklist names
7	      the gap, search_more_evidence fires a real scoped query, new rows fold in with
8	      collision-free ids + S2 stamps, the outline mutates (ev_ids grow / new gap-covering rows
9	      cited).
10	
11	  (b) SATURATED — a narrow question whose seed evidence already fully covers it (negative
12	      control). Acceptance: retrieval stays at ZERO (no search_more_evidence tool call ever
13	      fires) — the checklist should find nothing to search for and finish_outline should be
14	      accepted quickly.
15	
16	FAIL LOUD: any exception is allowed to propagate and print a full traceback — this harness never
17	swallows an error to fake a pass (LAW II).
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import asyncio
22	import json
23	import os
24	import sys
25	import time
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	# PORTABLE COPY of acceptance_outline_agent.py (the four /workspace/... hardcodes replaced with
29	# repo-relative / env-var-configurable paths). This file lives at <repo>/tests/oracle/, so the
30	# repo root is three parents up. Behaviour of the harness is otherwise byte-identical to the
31	# original — only path resolution changed, so it can serve as the governing oracle from any
32	# checkout location. See ORACLE_PORTABILITY note at the bottom for the four substitutions.
33	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
34	
35	# (1) repo root on sys.path — same intent as the original's dirname(dirname(__file__)), corrected
36	#     for this file's deeper location so `import src.polaris_graph...` resolves.
37	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
38	# (2) the original second sys.path.insert("/workspace/outline_agent_wt") pointed at the SAME repo
39	#     root under its old container layout (the outline-agent worktree WAS the repo). It is
40	#     redundant with (1) now that the repo root is resolved from __file__; overridable via
41	#     PG_EXTRA_SYS_PATH for an out-of-tree checkout.
42	_extra = os.environ.get("PG_EXTRA_SYS_PATH")
43	if _extra:
44	    sys.path.insert(0, _extra)
45	
46	from dotenv import load_dotenv  # noqa: E402
47	# (3) .env location: env override PG_DOTENV wins; else the repo-local .env; else the original
48	#     /workspace/POLARIS/.env if it still exists. Missing .env is non-fatal — the process env
49	#     (e.g. an exported OPENROUTER_API_KEY / SERPER_API_KEY) is used as-is.
50	_dotenv_candidates = [
51	    os.environ.get("PG_DOTENV"),
52	    str(_REPO_ROOT / ".env"),
53	    "/workspace/POLARIS/.env",
54	]
55	for _dp in _dotenv_candidates:
56	    if _dp and Path(_dp).is_file():
57	        load_dotenv(_dp, override=True)
58	        print(f"[portable] loaded dotenv from {_dp}")
59	        break
60	else:
61	    print("[portable] no .env file found; relying on the process environment for provider keys")
62	
63	os.environ["PG_OUTLINE_AGENT"] = "1"
64	os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS", "131072")
65	os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "32768")
66	# Keep the acceptance run FAST: small turn/wall budgets are fine for W1 proof (the design's
67	# defaults are 24 turns / 900s for a full production run; the smoke here just needs the loop to
68	# fire at least once on the thin case and zero times on the saturated case).
69	os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS", "6")
70	os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS", "420")
71	
72	from src.polaris_graph.outline import outline_agent as oa  # noqa: E402
73	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import run_live_retrieval  # noqa: E402
74	
75	
76	def _bootstrap_seed(query: str, max_serper: int = 6) -> list[dict]:
77	    """A real, live, narrow-scoped retrieval call used ONLY to bootstrap a small realistic seed
78	    pool for the harness (not part of the agent loop itself)."""
79	    result = run_live_retrieval(
80	        research_question=query, max_serper=max_serper, fetch_cap=10, anchor_seed=True,
81	    )
82	    rows = list(result.evidence_rows or [])
83	    print(f"[bootstrap] query={query!r} -> {len(rows)} evidence rows "
84	          f"(fetched={result.candidates_fetched}, failed={result.candidates_failed_fetch})")
85	    return rows
86	
87	
88	async def run_thin() -> dict:
89	    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
90	    print("THIN RUN — seed covers only 'efficacy', question also asks 'long-term CV safety'")
91	    print("=" * 80)
92	    seed_rows = _bootstrap_seed(
93	        "tirzepatide 15mg HbA1c reduction efficacy SURPASS trial results", max_serper=6,
94	    )
95	    if len(seed_rows) < 3:
96	        raise RuntimeError(f"bootstrap seed too thin to test with ({len(seed_rows)} rows) — "
97	                            "live retrieval may be degraded; not faking a pass")
98	
99	    question = (
100	        "What is the HbA1c-lowering efficacy of tirzepatide 15mg AND what is known about its "
101	        "long-term cardiovascular safety (major adverse cardiovascular events, SURMOUNT-MMO "
102	        "or equivalent outcome trial data)?"
103	    )
104	    evidence = list(seed_rows)
105	    ev_before = {r.get("evidence_id") for r in evidence if isinstance(r, dict)}
106	
107	    t0 = time.monotonic()
108	    parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok = await oa.run_outline_agent_or_legacy(
109	        question, evidence, oa.outliner_code_model(), 0.2, 131072,
110	    )
111	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
112	
113	    stats = parse_result.digest_stats.get("outline_agent", {})
114	    seed_ev_by_title = {t: set(v) for t, v in stats.get("seed_ev_by_title", {}).items()}
115	    final_ev_by_title = {t: set(v) for t, v in stats.get("final_ev_by_title", {}).items()}
116	    outline_mutated = (
117	        set(seed_ev_by_title.keys()) != set(final_ev_by_title.keys())
118	        or any(
119	            seed_ev_by_title.get(t, set()) != final_ev_by_title.get(t, set())
120	            for t in final_ev_by_title
121	        )
122	    )
123	    update_outline_calls = sum(
124	        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("update_outline:")
125	    )
126	    print(f"\n--- THIN RUN RESULT (elapsed {elapsed:.1f}s) ---")
127	    print(f"outline_mutated={outline_mutated} update_outline_calls={update_outline_calls}")
128	    print(f"seed_ev_by_title={ {t: len(v) for t, v in seed_ev_by_title.items()} }")
129	    print(f"final_ev_by_title={ {t: len(v) for t, v in final_ev_by_title.items()} }")
130	    print(f"turns={stats.get('turns')} ev_before={len(ev_before)} "
131	          f"ev_after={stats.get('ev_store_size')} new_evidence={stats.get('new_evidence_count')}")
132	    print("gap_ledger:")
133	    for t in stats.get("gap_ledger", []):
134	        print(f"  {t}")
135	    print("disclosures:")
136	    for d in stats.get("disclosures", []):
137	        print(f"  - {d}")
138	    print("final outline plans:")
139	    for p in parse_result.plans:
140	        print(f"  - {oa._plan_field(p, 'title', '')!r}: "  # noqa: SLF001 — harness, same-repo reuse
141	              f"{len(oa._plan_field(p, 'ev_ids', []) or [])} ev_ids")
142	
143	    search_calls = sum(
144	        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("search_more_evidence[")
145	    )
146	    checklist_gaps = sum(
147	        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("checklist[")
148	    )
149	    return {
150	        "elapsed_s": round(elapsed, 1),
151	        "turns": stats.get("turns"),
152	        "ev_before": len(ev_before),
153	        "ev_after": stats.get("ev_store_size"),
154	        "new_evidence_count": stats.get("new_evidence_count"),
155	        "search_more_evidence_calls": search_calls,
156	        "checklist_gap_events": checklist_gaps,
157	        "unfilled_gaps": stats.get("unfilled_gaps"),
158	        "outline_mutated": outline_mutated,
159	        "update_outline_calls": update_outline_calls,
160	        "final_section_ev_counts": {
161	            oa._plan_field(p, "title", ""): len(oa._plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])  # noqa: SLF001
162	            for p in parse_result.plans
163	        },
164	        "disclosures": stats.get("disclosures"),
165	    }
166	
167	
168	async def run_saturated() -> dict:
169	    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
170	    print("SATURATED RUN (negative control) — VALID multi-section outline, fully-covered facets")
171	    print("=" * 80)
172	    # Iter-2 fix (Fable verdict: the prior single-fact question was TOO narrow — the seed outline
173	    # came back below the 3-section floor, `run_outline_agent_or_legacy` early-returned BEFORE the
174	    # agent loop was ever constructed, and the checklist's anti-invention behavior was never
175	    # exercised at all. That "pass" was vacuous.). This question is deliberately THREE explicit,
176	    # separately-named facets — construction history, structural engineering design, and cultural
177	    # significance — so the seed outline is a genuine >=3-section plan and the agent loop actually
178	    # runs. Each facet gets its OWN thorough bootstrap query so the seed pool is rich enough that a
179	    # correctly-grounded checklist (the P0-1 verbatim-quote gate, outline_agent.py `_run_checklist`)
180	    # has nothing real left to flag — this exercises the SAME anti-invention gate the thin run
181	    # exercises the opposite side of, on a genuinely-built loop, not a skipped one.
182	    seed_rows_history = _bootstrap_seed(
183	        "Eiffel Tower construction history 1887 1889 timeline Gustave Eiffel build phases",
184	        max_serper=8,
185	    )
186	    seed_rows_engineering = _bootstrap_seed(
187	        "Eiffel Tower structural engineering design wrought iron lattice wind resistance load",
188	        max_serper=8,
189	    )
190	    seed_rows_culture = _bootstrap_seed(
191	        "Eiffel Tower French cultural identity symbol Paris landmark significance tourism",
192	        max_serper=8,
193	    )
194	    seen_urls: set[str] = set()
195	    seed_rows: list[dict] = []
196	    for r in seed_rows_history + seed_rows_engineering + seed_rows_culture:
197	        u = str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "")
198	        if u and u in seen_urls:
199	            continue
200	        if u:
201	            seen_urls.add(u)
202	        seed_rows.append(r)
203	    if len(seed_rows) < 9:
204	        raise RuntimeError(
205	            f"bootstrap seed too thin for a genuine 3-facet saturated control "
206	            f"({len(seed_rows)} rows across 3 facets) — live retrieval may be degraded; "
207	            "not faking a pass"
208	        )
209	
210	    question = (
211	        "Describe the construction history of the Eiffel Tower, its structural engineering "
212	        "design, and its role in French cultural identity."
213	    )
214	    evidence = list(seed_rows)
215	    ev_before = {r.get("evidence_id") for r in evidence if isinstance(r, dict)}
216	    t0 = time.monotonic()
217	    parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok = await oa.run_outline_agent_or_legacy(
218	        question, evidence, oa.outliner_code_model(), 0.2, 131072,
219	    )
220	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
221	
222	    stats = parse_result.digest_stats.get("outline_agent", {})
223	    print(f"\n--- SATURATED RUN RESULT (elapsed {elapsed:.1f}s) ---")
224	    print(f"turns={stats.get('turns')} ev_before={len(ev_before)} "
225	          f"ev_after={stats.get('ev_store_size')} new_evidence={stats.get('new_evidence_count')}")
226	    print("gap_ledger:")
227	    for t in stats.get("gap_ledger", []):
228	        print(f"  {t}")
229	    print("disclosures:")
230	    for d in stats.get("disclosures", []):
231	        print(f"  - {d}")
232	
233	    search_calls = sum(
234	        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("search_more_evidence[")
235	    )
236	    # "checklist[seed]" now covers BOTH the "named N gap(s)" and the "ran: NONE" disclosure
237	    # forms (outline_agent.py iter-2 telemetry fix) — either one proves the checklist LLM call
238	    # actually happened, distinct from the loop never reaching it.
239	    checklist_ran = any(d.startswith("checklist[seed]") for d in stats.get("disclosures", []))
240	    finish_accepted = any(
241	        d.startswith("finish_outline ACCEPTED") for d in stats.get("disclosures", [])
242	    )
243	    plans_nonempty = len(parse_result.plans) > 0
244	    zero_searches = search_calls == 0
245	    section_count = len(parse_result.plans)
246	    # Iter-2 fix: a `stats=={}` (seed-outline-invalid) early-return is NO LONGER accepted as a
247	    # pass path for THIS test. Fable's iter-2 verdict was explicit: that outcome is a degenerate
248	    # "the loop was never built" result, not evidence the checklist declines to invent gaps on a
249	    # VALID outline. This harness now REQUIRES the full agent loop to have actually run — a
250	    # 3-facet seed engineered to produce >=3 real sections (see bootstrap above) — and only then
251	    # checks that the checklist, when genuinely invoked against fully-covered facets, still
252	    # returns nothing to search for. If the seed STILL comes back invalid despite the 3-facet
253	    # design, that is reported honestly as a harness/seed problem (`seed_outline_invalid_early_
254	    # return=True`) and `valid_negative_control=False` — it is NOT silently treated as a pass.
255	    seed_outline_invalid_early_return = not stats  # stats=={} iff the early-return branch fired
256	    full_loop_ran = (
257	        plans_nonempty and section_count >= 3 and stats.get("turns", 0) >= 1
258	        and checklist_ran and finish_accepted
259	    )
260	    valid_negative_control = zero_searches and full_loop_ran
261	    print(f"valid_negative_control={valid_negative_control} "
262	          f"(full_loop_ran={full_loop_ran}, seed_outline_invalid_early_return="
263	          f"{seed_outline_invalid_early_return}, plans_nonempty={plans_nonempty}, "
264	          f"section_count={section_count} (>=3 required), turns>=1={stats.get('turns', 0) >= 1}, "
265	          f"checklist_ran={checklist_ran}, finish_accepted={finish_accepted}, "
266	          f"zero_searches={zero_searches} [search_calls={search_calls}])")
267	    if seed_outline_invalid_early_return:
268	        print(f"  DEGENERATE (not a pass): seed outline reason_codes="
269	              f"{getattr(parse_result, 'reason_codes', None)}, ok={getattr(parse_result, 'ok', None)}")
270	
271	    return {
272	        "elapsed_s": round(elapsed, 1),
273	        "turns": stats.get("turns"),
274	        "ev_before": len(ev_before),
275	        "ev_after": stats.get("ev_store_size"),
276	        "new_evidence_count": stats.get("new_evidence_count"),
277	        "search_more_evidence_calls": search_calls,
278	        "section_count": section_count,
279	        "full_loop_ran": full_loop_ran,
280	        "valid_negative_control": valid_negative_control,
281	        "seed_outline_invalid_early_return": seed_outline_invalid_early_return,
282	        "seed_reason_codes": (
283	            list(getattr(parse_result, "reason_codes", []) or [])
284	            if seed_outline_invalid_early_return else None
285	        ),
286	        "gap_ledger": stats.get("gap_ledger"),
287	        "disclosures": stats.get("disclosures"),
288	    }
289	
290	
291	async def main() -> None:
292	    # Iter-2: allow re-verifying a single scenario (``--only thin`` / ``--only saturated``) so a
293	    # targeted fix to one path doesn't force re-spending on the other, already-passing scenario.
294	    # Default (no arg) runs BOTH, unchanged, and writes the full acceptance_result.json.
295	    only = None
296	    if "--only" in sys.argv:
297	        idx = sys.argv.index("--only")
298	        if idx + 1 < len(sys.argv):
299	            only = sys.argv[idx + 1].strip().lower()
300	
301	    result: dict = {}
302	    if only in (None, "thin"):
303	        result["thin"] = await run_thin()
304	    if only in (None, "saturated"):
305	        result["saturated"] = await run_saturated()
306	    print("\n" + "#" * 80)
307	    print("ACCEPTANCE SUMMARY")
308	    print("#" * 80)
309	    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))
310	    # (4) output dir: env override PG_ACCEPTANCE_OUT_DIR wins; else the repo root (mirrors the
311	    #     original which wrote into /workspace/outline_agent_wt, i.e. the repo root of the old
312	    #     container layout). Dir is created if absent.
313	    out_dir = Path(os.environ.get("PG_ACCEPTANCE_OUT_DIR", str(_REPO_ROOT)))
314	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
315	    out_name = "acceptance_result.json" if only is None else f"acceptance_result_{only}.json"
316	    out_path = out_dir / out_name
317	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
318	        json.dump(result, fh, indent=2, default=str)
319	    print(f"[portable] wrote {out_path}")
320	
321	
322	if __name__ == "__main__":
323	    asyncio.run(main())
324

content.text:
Now I understand the structure. Let me make two changes: add the `valid_positive_control` computation and assertion in `run_thin()`, and wire non-zero exit into `main()`.

First, the `run_thin()` change — add the boolean before the return and include it in the returned dict.

input.old_string:
search_calls = sum(
        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("search_more_evidence[")
    )
    checklist_gaps = sum(
        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("checklist[")
    )
    return {
        "elapsed_s": round(elapsed, 1),
        "turns": stats.get("turns"),
        "ev_before": len(ev_before),
        "ev_after": stats.get("ev_store_size"),
        "new_evidence_count": stats.get("new_evidence_count"),
        "search_more_evidence_calls": search_calls,
        "checklist_gap_events": checklist_gaps,
        "unfilled_gaps": stats.get("unfilled_gaps"),
        "outline_mutated": outline_mutated,
input.new_string:
search_calls = sum(
        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("search_more_evidence[")
    )
    checklist_gaps = sum(
        1 for d in stats.get("disclosures", []) if d.startswith("checklist[")
    )
    # Enforceable POSITIVE control (mirrors run_saturated's valid_negative_control): the thin case
    # MUST have fired at least one real scoped retrieval AND mutated the outline vs the seed. If
    # either is false the harness is asserting nothing and the control is vacuous — fail loud.
    valid_positive_control = search_calls >= 1 and outline_mutated
    print(f"valid_positive_control={valid_positive_control} "
          f"(search_calls>=1={search_calls >= 1} [search_calls={search_calls}], "

input.new_string:
with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(result, fh, indent=2, default=str)
    print(f"[portable] wrote {out_path}")

    # Enforce BOTH controls: the harness must exit NON-ZERO if the positive (THIN) control did not
    # fire a search + mutate the outline, or if the negative (SATURATED) control did not stay at
    # zero retrieval on a genuinely-built loop. A scenario that was not run this invocation
    # (``--only``) is skipped, not treated as a failure.
    failures: list[str] = []
    if "thin" in result and not result["thin"].get("valid_positive_control"):
        failures.append("positive control (THIN): valid_positive_control=False")
    if "saturated" in result and not result["saturated"].get("valid_negative_control"):
        failures.append("negative control (SATURATED): valid_negative_control=False")
    if failures:
        print("\nACCEPTANCE FAILED:")
        for f in failures:
            print(f"  - {f}")
        sys.exit(1)
    print("\nACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
